introSection: Yukon Striker is a steel dive hyper roller coaster located at Canada's Wonderland in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada. The ride was designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and manufactured in Ohio. It opened to the public on May 3, 2019. Yukon Striker is the largest Dive Coaster installation built to date. It was constructed within the new Frontier Canada themed area, which is based on the Yukon backcountry during the Klondike Gold Rush in the late 1890s.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In January 2018, Bolliger & Mabillard track was spotted from the air at Canada's Wonderland. Due to the size of the track, this was presumed to be for a Dive Coaster in 2019. Later that month, a permit was filed revealing Canada's Wonderland's plans to install a roller coaster with an underwater tunnel.

In March 2018, an underground tunnel began to be dug underneath Vortex, the park's Arrow Dynamics suspended roller coaster.

In May 2018, teasers appeared that hinted at a gold rush theme for the ride. In July, the park set an August 15 date for the ride's announcement. A promotional video revealing the name Yukon Striker and the layout was published by CBC the night before the announcement. This was confirmed at the official unveiling, during which further details were revealed.

In February 2019, the ride's track layout was completed.

On March 14, 2019, the coaster completed its first test run.

The coaster opened as planned on May 3, 2019.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Yukon Striker is a Dive Coaster manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard. It features a 122 foot tall vertical loop, a first for the ride type, and an underground tunnel passing beneath Vortex. It also features a 152 foot tall zero-g winder, a new type of inversion. While its height is the same as that of Valravn at Cedar Point, Yukon Striker has a taller drop and a longer track length.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Yukon Striker makes a 180 degree right turn out of the station, dipping and rising into the lift hill. It turns 90 degrees to the right at the apex, hanging over the 24 story drop. After dropping, it enters an underwater tunnel into the 187 foot tall immelmann. This is followed by a 152 foot tall zero-g winder to the right, into a 122 foot tall vertical loop, followed by a 103 foot tall immelmann over Timberwolf Falls. The mid course brake run is also located over Timberwolf Falls, and the second drop leads into a helix underneath the first drop which goes into the final brake run.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Yukon Striker has copper track and aqua green supports.

fullText:
Yukon Striker is a steel dive hyper roller coaster located at Canada's Wonderland in Vaughan, Ontario, Canada. The ride was designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and manufactured in Ohio. It opened to the public on May 3, 2019. Yukon Striker is the largest Dive Coaster installation built to date. It was constructed within the new Frontier Canada themed area, which is based on the Yukon backcountry during the Klondike Gold Rush in the late 1890s.

In January 2018, Bolliger & Mabillard track was spotted from the air at Canada's Wonderland. Due to the size of the track, this was presumed to be for a Dive Coaster in 2019. Later that month, a permit was filed revealing Canada's Wonderland's plans to install a roller coaster with an underwater tunnel.

In March 2018, an underground tunnel began to be dug underneath Vortex, the park's Arrow Dynamics suspended roller coaster.

In May 2018, teasers appeared that hinted at a gold rush theme for the ride. In July, the park set an August 15 date for the ride's announcement. A promotional video revealing the name Yukon Striker and the layout was published by CBC the night before the announcement. This was confirmed at the official unveiling, during which further details were revealed.

In February 2019, the ride's track layout was completed.

On March 14, 2019, the coaster completed its first test run.

The coaster opened as planned on May 3, 2019.

Yukon Striker is a Dive Coaster manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard. It features a 122 foot tall vertical loop, a first for the ride type, and an underground tunnel passing beneath Vortex. It also features a 152 foot tall zero-g winder, a new type of inversion. While its height is the same as that of Valravn at Cedar Point, Yukon Striker has a taller drop and a longer track length.

Yukon Striker makes a 180 degree right turn out of the station, dipping and rising into the lift hill. It turns 90 degrees to the right at the apex, hanging over the 24 story drop. After dropping, it enters an underwater tunnel into the 187 foot tall immelmann. This is followed by a 152 foot tall zero-g winder to the right, into a 122 foot tall vertical loop, followed by a 103 foot tall immelmann over Timberwolf Falls. The mid course brake run is also located over Timberwolf Falls, and the second drop leads into a helix underneath the first drop which goes into the final brake run.

Yukon Striker has copper track and aqua green supports.
